evals.prompt: You are executing /prd-implement for PRD #5000. The lowest open wave is W2 with AFK child issue #5101, "Add tenant-scoped route guard." The parent PRD issue still shows `- [ ] W2 - #5101 Add tenant-scoped route guard - AFK - blocked by #5099`. The child issue's Verification section says to run: (1) `pnpm exec expo-doctor`, (2) `pnpm exec expo-router verify`, and (3) `pnpm test -- route-guard.test.ts`. Implementation and review are already complete. In verification, `pnpm test -- route-guard.test.ts` passes, but `pnpm exec expo-router verify` fails immediately with `/bin/sh: expo-router: command not found`. A subagent proposes using manual code inspection plus `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` instead because it should be good enough. Produce the controller's exact user-facing response and issue-handling outcome.
evals.expected_output: The controller refuses weaker substitute checks, reports the missing framework CLI precisely, keeps the child issue open, and stops before advancing to another wave.
expectations: Names the missing required verification command or missing `expo-router` CLI as the blocker.
Explicitly rejects substituting static analysis, code inspection, or `tsc --noEmit` for the required verification command.
Keeps the child issue open or not ready to close.
Stops before the next wave or other new work proceeds.

evals.prompt: You are executing /prd-implement for PRD #5000. Ready wave W3 contains only AFK child issue #5104, "Add admin settings form." The parent PRD issue still shows `- [ ] W3 - #5104 Add admin settings form - AFK - blocked by #5103`. The implementation subagent returns a clean RED -> GREEN -> REFACTOR summary, changed files, and these command results: `npm test -- settings-form.test.tsx` passed, `pnpm exec next typegen` could not run because `next` is unavailable in this checkout, so the subagent skipped it and instead read the generated types file plus ran `pnpm exec eslint app/settings/form.tsx`. Show how the controller handles the checkpoint before review.
evals.expected_output: The controller checkpoint catches the skipped required verification command, rejects the weaker substitutes, keeps the issue open, and stops before review starts.
expectations: Stops at the controller checkpoint before review rather than proceeding into review.
Identifies the skipped or unavailable `pnpm exec next typegen` command as the problem.
Treats reading generated files or running eslint as insufficient substitutes for the required verification command.
Keeps child issue #5104 open or not ready to close.

evals.prompt: You are executing /prd-implement for PRD #6000. The lowest open wave is W4. The parent PRD issue still shows `- [ ] W4 - #6101 CSV export filters - AFK - blocked by #6099` and `- [ ] W4 - #6102 CSV export download audit log - AFK - blocked by #6101`. AFK child issues #6101, "CSV export filters," and #6102, "CSV export download audit log," finished implementation and review. The controller's verification plan uses one shared command group for both issues because each child issue says verification is `bundle exec rspec spec/requests/admin/export_spec.rb`. The verification subagent reports `/bin/sh: bundle: command not found`. Another agent suggests closing #6101 anyway because its diff looks correct and leaving only #6102 open. Produce the controller's response.
evals.expected_output: The controller treats the shared verification command group as blocking both issues, rejects diff inspection as a substitute, keeps both child issues open, and stops before the next wave.
expectations: States that the shared verification command group's failure or missing tool blocks closure for both covered issues, not just one of them.
Names the missing `bundle` or `bundle exec rspec` command as the blocker.
Rejects diff inspection or 'looks satisfied' reasoning as a substitute for the required shared verification command.
Keeps child issues #6101 and #6102 open or not ready to close.
Stops before the next wave or any additional issue closure proceeds.

evals.prompt: You are executing /prd-implement for PRD #7000. AFK child issue #7101 is ready for verification. The parent PRD issue still shows `- [ ] W2 - #7101 Add user retry headers - AFK - blocked by #7098`. Its Verification section says: (1) `pnpm exec redwood test api/src/functions/users/users.test.ts`; (2) if the Redwood CLI is unavailable in the current environment, run `yarn rw test api/src/functions/users/users.test.ts` instead. The verification subagent reports `pnpm: command not found`, then runs `yarn rw test api/src/functions/users/users.test.ts` successfully. Show the controller response.
evals.expected_output: The controller accepts the fallback only because the issue explicitly lists it, records the verification evidence, moves the issue into explicit GitHub closeout without treating verification alone as completion, and leaves the parent PRD issue open.
expectations: Acknowledges that the original `pnpm` command could not run.
Uses or accepts the explicitly listed `yarn rw test` fallback command.
Makes clear that the fallback is acceptable because the child issue explicitly allowed it.
Moves child issue #7101 into explicit GitHub closeout after the fallback passes instead of treating verification alone as completion.
Makes clear that parent task-graph checkbox updates are not required for task completion.
Leaves the parent PRD issue open after closing #7101.

Treats child issue state as authoritative even though the parent PRD task-graph line is still unchecked.

evals.prompt: You are executing /prd-implement for PRD #9000. All AFK child issues are complete and already synchronized in the parent task graph. expectations: States that commit, push, or PR work is out of scope for this prd-implement run.
Explicitly says no PR will be opened and no commit or push will be performed in this run.
Says the changes remain local-only or uncommitted.
Directs the user to use `commit` or `commit-to-pr` only in a separate follow-up request after this skill finishes.
Keeps the parent PRD issue open or says only HITL work remains.

Stops before review, verification, issue closure, or PR creation proceeds.
Keeps child issue #9101 open or not ready to close.
